title: The complete herbalist, or, The people their own physicians, by the use of nature's remedies : showing the great curative properties of all herbs , gums, balsams, barks, flowers, and roots, how they should be prepared, when and under what influences selected, at what time gathered, and for what diseases administered : also separate treatises on food and drinks, clothing, exercise, the regulation of the passions, life, health and disease, longevity, medication, air and sunshine, bathing, sleep, etc. : also symptoms of prevalent diseases, special treatment in special cases, and a new and plain system of hygienic principles
creator: Brown, O. Phelps (Oliver Phelps)
